rows.description: Soluta Dno Rectori, Scholaribus et Officiariis.

rows.notes: Chosen 'Ad Test: Nativitatis' from gpt-5-mini as it matches the visual inspection more accurately than gemini-flash's 'Natitattis'. Both agreed on row type and empty amounts.

rows.description: Francisco Milman A.M. Scholari peregrin: nanti; a Testo Annunciationis 1772 ad idem festum 1773
rows.notes: Amounts and type agreed by all. Adopted gpt-5-mini's description as it better reflects the subtle abbreviations and slight fading/ink blotting, and its notes acknowledged the uncertainty. The amounts are clearly 6 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence from the image.
rows.english_description: Stipend paid to Francisco Milman A.M., a travelling (peregrinating) scholar, covering the period from the Annunciation 1772 to the Annunciation 1773.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid to Francisco Milman A.M., covering the period from Annunciation 1772 to Annunciation 1773, amounting to £6 13s 4d.

rows.notes: All extractors agreed on this total row, description, and amounts, including the half-penny fraction. Visually confirmed as a total with a clear half-penny mark.

rows.description: Soluta Scholari Cæsariensi — nulla nil nil
rows.notes: Adopted gpt-5-mini's description which includes the '— nulla nil nil' directly as it appears to the right of the description, making it an explicit part of the entry's text value. Visual inspection confirms 'Cæsariensi' with the ligature. The presence of 'nulla nil nil' in the amount columns justifies classifying this as an 'entry' (as in gemini-flash) despite zero value, as it records a specific transaction.
rows.english_description: No payment made to the Scholar of Caesarea (no sum recorded).
rows.english_description_with_amount: No payment was made to the Scholar of Caesarea (no amount recorded).
